message:
You get up to grab a glass of water… and there's your dog, right behind you. You head to the kitchen… they're there. You go to the bathroom and shut the door, and two furry paws appear underneath it. 🐶🚪 If your pup is your personal shadow, congratulations — you've got yourself a "Velcro dog."

So why do they stick to you like, well, Velcro? A big part of it is pure instinct. Dogs are pack animals, and to them, you're the pack — and in the wild, staying close to your group means safety. On top of that, many breeds, especially those in the herding and working groups, were bred for generations to do everything side-by-side with their humans, so togetherness is practically written into their DNA. And that bathroom obsession? It's one of the most intensely YOU-scented rooms in the whole house, which to a dog is downright fascinating.

The sweetest part: your dog isn't trying to invade your privacy (they have no idea that's even a thing 😅). They're following you simply because being near you feels good and safe. It's loyalty and love in its most literal form.

So fess up — does your dog escort you to the bathroom too? Tell me the most ridiculous place yours has followed you! 🐾💛
